title: Blue Screen / System Crash
cardText: Diagnose Windows blue screen crashes, stop errors, and system instability.
Check error codes, drivers, updates, memory, storage, system files, and hardware faults.
Separate software problems from hardware failures.
text: This guide is for Windows systems that display a blue screen, stop error, or crash unexpectedly.
Record any error messages before restarting.
Check recent changes, drivers, updates, memory, storage, and system files.
Escalate hardware faults when software troubleshooting does not resolve the issue.

checkBlueScreenCode.title: Check for a blue screen error code
body: Record any stop code or error message shown on the blue screen.
Examples include MEMORY_MANAGEMENT, CRITICAL_PROCESS_DIED, PAGE_FAULT_IN_NONPAGED_AREA, and IRQL_NOT_LESS_OR_EQUAL.
The stop code often provides the first clue.

checkRecentChanges.title: Check recent changes
body: Determine whether the issue started after installing hardware, drivers, updates, or software.
Recent changes often reveal the source of instability.

undoRecentChange.title: Undo recent change
body: Remove, disable, or roll back the recent change if safe to do so.
Restart the computer and monitor for additional crashes.

checkWindowsUpdates.title: Check recent Windows updates
body: Review update history.
Some crashes begin after driver or Windows updates.
Check whether the timing matches the start of the issue.

rollbackUpdate.title: Rollback update
body: The crash appears related to a recent update.
Roll back the update if appropriate and monitor stability.
Install replacement updates when available.

checkDrivers.title: Check drivers
body: Review recently updated drivers.
Graphics, storage, chipset, and network drivers are common causes of blue screens.
Update or roll back drivers as needed.

repairDriverIssue.title: Driver issue identified
body: A driver appears responsible for the crashes.
Update, reinstall, or roll back the driver.
Monitor system stability after the change.

checkSafeMode.title: Test Safe Mode
body: Boot Windows into Safe Mode.
Safe Mode loads a minimal driver set.
If crashes stop, a driver or software conflict is likely.

softwareConflict.title: Software or driver conflict likely
body: The system is stable in Safe Mode.
Investigate startup software, third-party drivers, antivirus products, and recently installed applications.

checkMemory.title: Check memory (RAM)
body: Run a memory diagnostic tool.
Faulty RAM is one of the most common causes of blue screens.
Test memory modules individually if necessary.

replaceMemory.title: Memory fault identified
body: Memory testing detected errors.
Replace the faulty RAM and retest system stability.

checkStorageHealth.title: Check storage health
body: Review SSD or HDD health information.
Storage corruption can trigger blue screens.
Check SMART data and event logs for disk-related errors.

replaceStorage.title: Storage fault identified
body: The storage device appears unreliable.
Back up important data immediately.
Replace the drive and restore or reinstall Windows.

checkSystemFiles.title: Check Windows system files
body: Corrupted operating system files can cause crashes.
Run SFC and DISM repairs if appropriate.
Restart the computer afterwards.

repairSystemFiles.title: Repair system files
body: System file corruption was detected.
Repair completed successfully.
Monitor the system for further crashes.

checkTemperatures.title: Check temperatures
body: Monitor CPU and GPU temperatures.
Excessive heat can cause instability and blue screens.
Check for dust buildup and cooling issues.

resolveCoolingIssue.title: Cooling issue identified
body: The system is overheating.
Clean cooling components and improve airflow.
Retest after temperatures return to normal.

checkEventViewer.title: Review Event Viewer
body: Review System logs around the crash time.
Look for recurring warnings, driver failures, storage errors, or hardware events.
Document recurring patterns.

researchCrashCause.title: Investigate recurring error
body: Research the recurring event or stop code.
Apply any known fixes associated with the fault.

applySpecificFix.title: Apply targeted fix
body: Apply the documented fix associated with the error.
Retest stability afterwards.

checkFirmware.title: Check BIOS and firmware
body: Review motherboard, storage, and device firmware versions.
Outdated firmware can cause stability issues.
Install updates according to manufacturer guidance.

updateFirmware.title: Update firmware
body: Install available firmware updates.
Retest system stability afterwards.

hardwareEscalation.title: Advanced hardware diagnostics required
body: Drivers, updates, memory, storage, system files, temperatures, and firmware have been checked.
Possible causes include motherboard faults, CPU instability, GPU faults, power issues, or intermittent hardware failures.
Escalate for advanced diagnostics.
